channel.title: Naija Digest Online
channel.description: Nigeria’s one-stop hub for breaking news, tech, football, celebrity gossip, Naija music, live TV, exchange rates and trending topics.

item.title: Peller, Jarvis Announce August 1 Wedding Date
item.dc:creator: TVC News
item.description: TikTok stars Habeeb Hamzat, popularly known as Peller, and Amadou Elizabeth Aminata, better known as Jarvis, have announced August 1, 2026, as the date for their wedding. The couple made the announcement through a cinematic video shared across their social media platforms. The video concluded with t

item.title: Ochei Hails Ogunsakin, Adesina For Historic Wimbledon Breakthroughs
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: President of the Nigeria Tennis Federation (NTF), Rt Hon. Victor Ochei, has congratulated Nigerian tennis prospects Seun Ogunsakin and Esther Adesina for their impressive performances at the 2026 Wimbledon Junior Championships, describing their achievements as a testament to resilience, ambition and

item.title: Tinubu Retains Shettima As Running Mate, Presidential, NASS Candidates Submission Ends
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The All Progressives Congress (APC) has retained Vice President Kashim Shettima as President Bola Tinubu’s running mate for the 2027 presidential election. This was confirmed during the submission of the completed nomination forms of Tinubu and Shettima to the APC National Working Committee (NWC) fo

item.title: Lagos court jails 67-year-old lawyer for environmental offences
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: By Olasunkanmi Akoni A Lagos State Special Offences (Mobile) Court has sentenced a 67-year-old lawyer, Taiwo Abidemi, to seven months’ imprisonment for violating the state’s environmental laws. Abidemi was convicted after pleading guilty to a three-count charge bordering on breach of pea

item.title: Peller, Jarvis Announce August 1 White Wedding After Traditional Marriage
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Popular Nigerian TikTok stars Peller and Jarvis have officially announced that their white wedding will take place on August 1, 2026, following the completion of their traditional marriage rites. The couple, whose real names are Hamzat Habeeb Adelaja and Amadou Elizabeth Aminata, shared the exciting

item.title: Ondo APC Primaries: Fadola alleges candidate imposition, backs NWC intervention
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: A Chieftain of the All Progressives Congress, APC, in Ondo State, Emmanuel Fadola, has disclosed that the primaries of the party that took place in the state were characterised by authoritarian conduct by certain person who plotted to impose candidates on party members. Fadola, who appealed to membe

item.title: 2027: Tinubu Moves To Break Presidential Race Jinx In South East
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: JAMES KWEN examines ways and political moves by President Bola Ahmed Tinubu and his political party, the All Progressives Congress, to break a 13-year defeat of the party in the South-East region of the country come 2027. Since the All Progressives Congress (APC) emerged as a political party in 2013

item.title: PDP confirms uploading of presidential, NASS candidates on INEC portal
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: The National Working Committee (NWC) of the Peoples Democratic Party (PDP) has confirmed the successful uploading of the names of its presidential candidate and the candidates for National Assembly positions to the portal of the Independent National Electoral Commission (INEC) ahead of the 2027 poll

item.title: Alavin retracts viral allegation against Wizkid associate Godson, urges public to disregard video
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Emmanuel Jakpa, popularly known as Alavin, has retracted a viral video linked to the controversy involving businessman and social media influencer, Uyi Godson Agho, popularly known as Godson, urging members of the public to disregard its contents. Alavin’s statement comes days after Godson, an assoc

item.title: Oyo abduction: CAN celebrates rescue of kidnapped Oriire pupils, teachers
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: ​The CAN President also applauded Gov. Seyi Makinde of Oyo State for his steadfast support to the affected families and his administration’s commitment to rehabilitating the rescued victims. The post Oyo abduction: CAN celebrates rescue of kidnapped Oriire pupils, teachers appeared first on Vanguard

item.title: Anti-corruption groups urge African leaders to close enforcement gap, back international anti-graft court
item.dc:creator: Premium Times
item.description: The groups made the call on Saturday to mark the 2026 African Anti-Corruption Day observed under the African Union (AU) theme, "Scaling Up the Promotion of Integrity and Anti-Corruption Actions Across Africa." The post Anti-corruption groups urge African leaders to close enforcement gap, back intern

item.title: Novacrust: FX costs eroding earnings for Africa’s remote workers

item.description: Financial technology company Novacrust has raised concerns over the high cost of charges that remote workers and digital professionals across Africa The post Novacrust: FX costs eroding earnings for Africa’s remote workers appeared first on Nairametrics.

item.title: Oyo Rescue: CAN Urges FG Not To Forget Nigerians Still In Captivity
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: The Christian Association of Nigeria (CAN) has said that while the successful rescue of pupils, teachers and other victims abducted from schools in Oriire Local Government Area of Oyo State offers hope, the Federal Government must sustain efforts until every Nigerian still held by terrorists, bandit

item.title: Beyoncé Blue Ivy Join Jay-Z at ‘Reasonable Doubt’ 30th Anniversary Concert
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: American music icon Jay-Z launched his three-night Reasonable Doubt 30th-anniversary concert series at Yankee Stadium on Friday, featuring special appearances by his wife, Beyoncé, and their daughter, Blue Ivy.   The opening show began with a prerecorded video of Beyoncé cutting the rapper&#821

item.title: Galatasaray Reach Agreement With Burnley For Ugochukwu
item.dc:creator: Completesports
item.description: Turkish Super Lig champions Galatasaray have reached an agreement with Sky Bet Championship side Burnley for the signing of Nigerian-born midfielder Lesley Ugochukwu. Transfer expert Fabrizio Romano confirmed that the two clubs have finalised the deal, with the midfielder now set to complete the for

item.dc:creator: Vanguard

The post Nine family members, two-month-old baby killed in fresh Plateau attack app

item.description: Manchester United legend Wayne Rooney believes Lionel Messi can be a defensive liability for Argentina ahead of Wednesday’s clash against England in the semi-final of the FIFA World Cup 2026. While serving as a pundit for the BBC Sport coverage of the 2026 World Cup, Rooney highlighted that Me

item.title: Peller says marriage counselling will make him a better husband
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Popular Nigerian content creator and TikTok personality, Hamzat Habeeb Adelaja, popularly known as Peller, has revealed that he is attending private marriage counselling sessions as part of preparations for his forthcoming white wedding to his wife, Amadou Elizabeth Aminata, widely known as Jarvis.

item.title: Transfer: Galatasaray set to announce deal for Ugochukwu
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Turkish champions Galatasaray have finalized an agreement with Burnley to sign Super Eagles-eligible midfielder Lesley Ugochukwu for a fee estimated at €30 million as reported by transfer market specialist Fabrizio Romano. The 22-year-old had a remarkable season with the Clarets in the Premier Leagu

item.title: Nine family members, two-month-old baby killed in fresh Plateau attack
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: Village head critically injured By Golok Nanmwa JOS — No fewer than nine members of the same family, including a two-month-old baby, were reportedly killed in a fresh attack by suspected gunmen on Kum and Wereng-Camp communities in Riyom Local Government Area of Plateau State late Saturday night. item.description: Nigerian professionals hoping to secure employment in the United States in 2026 may improve their chances by targeting companies with a strong record of sponsoring H-1B work visas. item.title: Borno students, groups rally behind security agencies in fight against terrorism
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: By Ndahi Marama, Maiduguri Students from tertiary institutions across Borno State, alongside civil society organisations, youth groups and community leaders, on Sunday staged a peaceful solidarity rally in support of Nigeria’s security agencies in the fight against Boko Haram, ISWAP, banditry

item.title: Nigerian govt mourns Emir of Qatar, Sheikh Hamad Al Thani
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: The Federal Government has expressed deep sorrow over the death of the Emir of Qatar, His Highness Sheikh Hamad bin Khalifa Al Thani, who died at the age of 74. Following his passing, Qatar declared a four-day period of national mourning beginning Monday. Government ministries and public instit

item.title: Nigeria Missed Global Maths Competition Over Lack Of Invitations — FG
item.dc:creator: The Whistler
item.description: The Federal Government has explained that Nigeria’s absence from the 2025 and 2026 editions of the International Mathematical Olympiad (IMO) was not due to lack of funding but because the country did not receive official invitation letters from the host countries. The clarification was given by the

item.title: Ponzi scheme victims can seek legal remedies — lawyers
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: Opara said it was wrong to attribute participation in Ponzi schemes to a lack of investment opportunities, noting that promoters often exploit investors’ greed through promises of unrealistic returns.

item.title: NDLEA Arrests S’African Woman With 5.75kg Of Heroin
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: The operatives of the National Drug Law Enforcement Agency (NDLEA) has arrested a 38-year-old South African woman, Will Ann at Nnamdi Azikiwe International Airport, Abuja, for allegedly attempting to smuggle 5.75 kilograms of heroin into Nigeria while travelling with her three-year-old son. item.description: As the United Kingdom-funded Partnership for Learning for All in Nigeria (PLANE) programme prepares to wind down after five years of implementation, the Kano State Government has pledged to sustain and institutionalise the reforms introduced under the initiative to ensure lasting improvements in the

item.description: Chairman of the Delta State Athletics Association, Sir Itiako Ikpokpo (KSM), has expressed delight over the selection of several standout athletes from the Niger Delta Games (NDG) to represent Team Nigeria at the 2026 Commonwealth Games in Glasgow, Scotland. Ikpokpo described the inclusion of the at

item.title: ECOWAS: Nigeria advocates PPPs as key to West Africa’s infrastructure needs
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Nigeria has urged ECOWAS members to embrace Public-Private Partnerships to bridge infrastructure gaps and drive sustainable economic growth. The post ECOWAS: Nigeria advocates PPPs as key to West Africa’s infrastructure needs appeared first on The Sun Nigeria.

item.dc:creator: Daily Post

Shehu made the remarks in a post shared on his X account on Saturday night while reacting to reports that no fewer than 16

Fayose gave the advice in a post on his official Instagram page while reacting to the recent appeal by the First Lady who called o

item.description: Abia State Governor, Dr. Alex Otti, has directed the immediate commencement of rehabilitation works at Ibeama Primary School, Ndume Otuka, the primary school attended by Nigeria’s former Military Head of State, late General Aguiyi Ironsi. The Governor also directed the Commissioner for Works, Engr.

item.title: EU, FG, UNICEF, UNFPA reaffirm commitment to build stronger healthcare system in Nigeria
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: The Federal Government of Nigeria has reaffirmed its commitment to partner the European Union (EU), the United Nations Children’s Fund (UNICEF) and other relevant agencies in building a stronger and more equitable health system that leaves no woman, child or adolescent behind. item.title: Police arrest 43 suspects, rescue six kidnap victims in nationwide operations

item.description: By Kingsley Omonobi The Nigeria Police Force has arrested 43 suspected criminals and rescued six kidnapped victims in coordinated operations carried out across the country within the last 24 hours. The Force Headquarters said the operations, conducted in collaboration with other security agencies, a

item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Switzerland were left fuming after forward Breel Embolo was sent off in the second half of Sunday’s World Cup quarter-final following a VAR review that deemed him guilty of simulation, a decision that proved pivotal in their 3-1 extra-time defeat to Argentina. item.title: Super Eagles transfers: Fiorentina plot move for 10-cap Nigeria star wanted by Trabzonspor

item.description: Serie A side Fiorentina have become the latest club to express interest in signing Super Eagles striker Tolu Arokodare from Wolves this summer, Soccernet.ng reports. Arokodare's future has remained uncertain since Wolves were relegated from the Premier League last season, with growing speculation ov

item.title: Rice in bed for nearly three days before Norway game - Tuchel
item.dc:creator: BBC Sport Football
item.description: England boss Thomas Tuchel says Declan Rice was in bed for most of the three days prior to the World Cup quarter-final win against Norway because of a sickness bug.

item.dc:creator: Daily Trust
item.description: The Gombe State chapter of the Peoples’ Democratic Party (PDP) has called for an independent and transparent investigation into the collapse of a section of the new State House of Assembly Complex. item.title: Trump pays tribute to Graham: ‘You’re a true American patriot who will be greatly missed!!!’
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: In a Truth Social post, Trump heaped praise on Graham — one of his closest confidants in Congress — saying that the staunch Republican was "one of the greatest people and Senators I have ever known."

item.title: Senegal sack Thiaw, technical crew after poor World Cup performance
item.dc:creator: Daily Trust
item.description: The Senegal Football Federation (FSF) has announced the sack of Pape Thiaw as coach of its Senegal national team. The Federation also announced that Thiaw’s entire technical crew have been relieved of their duties, following the meeting of its executive committee on Saturday. The decision to sack Th

Abbas Tajudeen, on Sunday congratulated Nigerians on the safe rescue of the teachers, pupils, and students kidnapped by terrorists in Oyo State, describing their safe return as “a pleasant close to a bad chapter in the country’s history.&

item.description: Sheikh Hamad's 18-year rule catapulted Qatar to prosperity and reshaped its domestic, regional and global footprint.

Igboho said the government under Tinubu is determined to deal with all forms of cri

item.title: NPFL: Imoh Obot leaves Abia Warriors for Rivers United
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Rivers United have completed the signing of Imoh Obot for the 2026-27 season, DAILY POST reports. Obot teamed up with the Pride of Rivers from Abia Warriors. He managed only 11 appearances for Abia Warriors last season. Obot had previously featured for the nine-time Nigeria Premier Football League,

item.title: Former Flying Eagles Coach Takes Over At Sudan’s Al Ahli Madani
item.dc:creator: Completesports
item.description: Aliyu Zubairu has been appointed new head coach of Sudanese club Al Ahli Madani on a two-year contract, Completesports.com reports. Zubairu was recently the head coach of Egyptian second division club Telecom SC. The 62-year-old takes over from Sudanese coach Farouk Jabra, who left the club followin

item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: The Nigeria Security and Civil Defence Corps (NSCDC) has called on Nigerians to support security agencies with timely and credible information to strengthen the fight against terrorism and other forms of criminality across the country. This was contained in a statement signed by the National Public

item.dc:creator: TVC News
item.description: Senegal have dismissed head coach Pape Thiaw following the Teranga Lions’ elimination from the Round of 32 at the 2026 FIFA World Cup, according to a report by the BBC. Thiaw, who was appointed Senegal manager in December 2024, leaves after overseeing the country’s disappointing World Cu

item.title: The week in pictures: Le Pen's comeback, Khamenei's funeral and Mbappé's wondergoal

item.description: French far-right leader Marine Le Pen was cleared to run in next year's presidential election, despite having her embezzlement conviction confirmed, in a week that saw Iran hold days-long funeral ceremonies for slain supreme leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei and football stars Kylian Mbappé and Lionel M

item.title: Malaria linked to 5% of fever cases as Lagos declares malaria tests mandatory
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Lagos has made malaria testing mandatory, saying only about 5% of fever cases are malaria and urging treatment based on confirmed diagnosis. The post Malaria linked to 5% of fever cases as Lagos declares malaria tests mandatory appeared first on The Sun Nigeria.

item.title: NDLEA Arrests Ex-Okada Rider For Allegedly Smuggling 100 Wraps of Methamphetamine
item.dc:creator: TVC News
item.description: Operatives of the National Drug Law Enforcement Agency (NDLEA) have arrested a former commercial motorcyclist in Lagos after he allegedly ingested and excreted 100 wraps of methamphetamine in an attempt to smuggle the illicit drug into Nigeria. The arrest was disclosed in a statement issued on Sunda

item.title: Vieira Emerges Frontrunner For Senegal Coaching Job
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Patrick Vieira has emerged as the leading candidate to become Senegal’s next head coach following the team’s elimination from the 2026 FIFA World Cup, according to French newspaper L’Equipe. The Senegalese Football Federation has begun planning for the national team’s future

item.title: U.S. Senator Lindsey Graham Diies At 71
item.dc:creator: Information Nigeria
item.description: Prominent United States Senator Lindsey Graham, a key ally of President Donald Trump, has died at the age of 71 following a “brief and sudden illness,” his office has announced. The Republican senator from South Carolina’s office said in a statement on his official X account that Graham died on the

item.title: Nigeria Condoles Qatar Over Death Of Former Emir Sheikh Hamad
item.dc:creator: Channels TV
item.description: Nigeria described the late leader as a visionary statesman whose legacy extended far beyond Qatar's borders. The post Nigeria Condoles Qatar Over Death Of Former Emir Sheikh Hamad appeared first on Channels Television.

item.title: INEC extend candidates list submission deadline on IPAC appeal
item.dc:creator: BusinessDay
item.description: The Independent National Electoral Commission (INEC) on Sunday early morning announced the extension of deadline for the submission of candidates read more INEC extend candidates list submission deadline on IPAC appeal

item.title: CAN to FG: Don’t forget Nigerians still in captivity after Oyo children rescue
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: CAN urges federal government to intensify efforts to rescue Nigerians still in captivity following the release of abducted pupils in Oyo. The post CAN to FG: Don’t forget Nigerians still in captivity after Oyo children rescue appeared first on The Sun Nigeria.

item.title: US rapper Pitbull sets bald cap world record at London show
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: US rapper Pitbull has set the first Guinness World Record for the largest gathering of people wearing bald caps.

item.title: Former Leader Of Qatar Hamad Bin Khalifa Dies At 74
item.dc:creator: Channels TV
item.description: The former leader was seen as one of the key architects of modern Qatar and led the country during a period of rapid economic growth. The post Former Leader Of Qatar Hamad Bin Khalifa Dies At 74 appeared first on Channels Television.

item.title: TikTokers Jarvis, Peller Announce Wedding Date
item.dc:creator: Information Nigeria
item.description: Jarvis and Peller, popular Nigerian TikTok content creators, have announced that they will tie the knot on Aug. 1. The announcement was made through a cinematic video shared on their social media platforms, ending with the inscription “Jarvis & Peller 01-08-2026.” The video has since gone viral,

item.title: Eiffel Tower to close early as heatwave hits France: operator
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: The operator of the Eiffel Tower said the latticed-steel Paris monument would "exceptionally close" early over the weekend at 4 pm (1400 GMT), as France was gripped by a third heatwave since May.

item.title: Residents Debunk Flood Death Reports, Say Only Domestic Animals Were Lost
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: Residents of the Nkemba Street axis in Uyo, Akwa Ibom State, one of the communities benefiting from the state’s flood intervention programme, have dismissed reports circulating in some national newspapers and online platforms claiming that two people died during the recent flash floods trigger

item.title: Obi Commends Cardoso-Led CBN Monetary Policy Reforms
item.dc:creator: The Whistler
item.description: …Says Fiscal Policies Fuelling Economic Crisis The National Democratic Congress presidential candidate for the 2027 election, Mr Peter Obi, has commended the Central Bank of Nigeria (CBN) for what he described as its sound monetary management under Governor Olayemi Cardoso. However, he blamed weak f

item.title: NGX Reopens Trading In Thomas Wyatt Shares After Rule Compliance
item.dc:creator: The Whistler
item.description: The Nigerian Exchange Limited (NGX) has reopened trading in the shares of Thomas Wyatt Nigeria Plc after the company complied with its post-listing obligations by submitting its outstanding financial statements, bringing an end to a suspension that had been in place since October 2025. The Exchange

item.title: “A Maybach is good, but you can still help the poor” — Remi Tinubu to Burna Boy, Davido, Asake
item.dc:creator: Premium Times
item.description: “The burden on the government is huge; you can still help.” The post “A Maybach is good, but you can still help the poor” — Remi Tinubu to Burna Boy, Davido, Asake appeared first on Premium Times Nigeria.

item.title: U.S. Launches Third Strike On Iran
item.dc:creator: The Whistler
item.description: The United States has launched a third round of airstrikes against Iran this week after Iran’s Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) attacked a commercial ship transiting the Strait of Hormuz and declared the waterway closed. U.S. Central Command (CENTCOM) said the strikes began at about 7:

item.title: Drug trafficking: Man excretes 100 wraps of meth in Lagos
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: According to the statement, the suspect had worked as an okada rider in Lagos for 15 years before allegedly joining a transnational drug trafficking syndicate in a bid to improve his financial status.

item.title: Troops rescue 19 kidnap victims in two days, repel terrorists
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Operation FANSAN YAMMA troops rescued 19 kidnapped victims in two operations in Kebbi State, repelling terrorists without casualties. The post Troops rescue 19 kidnap victims in two days, repel terrorists appeared first on The Sun Nigeria.

item.title: Nigeria Gets Additional $208.3m World Bank Loan For Cash Transfer
item.dc:creator: Information Nigeria
item.description: The National Industrial Court in Abuja has ordered the deployment of at least one police lawyer to every police station across the country in line with the Police Act 2020. Justice O. Y. Anuwe gave the order on Friday while delivering judgment in a suit filed by the Incorporated Trustees of the Nige

item.title: Obi to Diaspora Africans: Your voices matter when things go wrong
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: The presidential candidate of the Nigeria Democratic Congress, NDC, Mr. Peter Obi, has urged Africans in the diaspora to remain actively involved in shaping the continent’s future, saying their voices are important whenever things go wrong in Africa. Obi made the call while delivering a keynote addr

item.title: Datacentres drive up big tech's carbon emissions to a third of those of France
item.dc:creator: Hacker News
item.description: Article URL: https://www.theguardian.com/us-news/2026/jul/11/microsoft-amazon-google-datacentre-carbon-emissions-france Comments URL: https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=48879950 Points: 10 # Comments: 8
